Description:
Ballistics of the 130mm F-44 (with mechanical base fuze 4MPM), ZS-44 (with mechanical time fuze DVM-60M1) and the ZS-44R (with AR-32 radio proximity fuze) rounds fired from A-217, A-218, and the A-222 "Bereg" gun mounts. Shell loads 3.56 kg of HE filler. Cartridge loads 10 kg of 15/7 pyroxylin propellant.

Work on the A-217 single-mount automatic gun started in 1967, and received the factory index ZIF-92 in 1969. Intended as the armament for Project 1135 Burevestnik class guard ship, it exceeded the weight allowance and was ultimately rejected. However, the same ballistics was reused for the twin automatic mount A-218 (factory index ZIF-94). Initially entering service with the Project 956 Sarych class, it was accepted for service in 1985 as the AK-130. It would also arm ships of the Project 1144 "Orlan" class and the Project 1164 "Slava" class.

Barrel length is 6990 mm, cartridge (total) length is 1364-1369 mm. Cartridge length is estimated at 770 mm from pixel measurement, pending authoritative sources. Projectile travel estimated at 6220 mm. Chambrage is estimated. From АРТИЛЛЕРИЙСКИЕ МЕТАТЕЛЬНЫЕ ЗАРЯДЫ, the following variation of performance with ambient condition is known:

T	mv	P.mcp
°C	m/s	MPa
-40	808	234
 15	850	300
 50	880	370

Pressure is interpreted as copper crusher gauge values and converted to piezo values with factor 1.12.

References:
- Энциклопедия отечественной артиллерии, А. Б. Широкорад; Под общ. ред. А.Е. Тараса., Минск : Харвест, 2000
- АРТИЛЛЕРИЙСКИЕ МЕТАТЕЛЬНЫЕ ЗАРЯДЫ, Г.Г. Валеев, В.Ф. Сопин, Б.А. Соков, ФГУП «Государственный научно-исследовательский институт химических продуктов», Казань, 2004
